intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good—I see the GSM5746268 triplet (barcodes, features, matrix.mtx.gz) plus two other samples. I'll focus on GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 as requested. Let me load the Seurat recipe first to ensure I follow the standard workflow:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Now let me present a plan for the workflow:

input.title: Seurat scRNA-seq workflow for GSM5746268
input.summary: Load 10x MTX triplet, QC, normalize, cluster, and identify markers in a standard Seurat v5 workflow.
input.rationale: Standard end-to-end scRNA-seq analysis with output UMAP and top markers per cluster.

steps.title: Load 10x MTX triplet and create Seurat object
steps.description: Read barcodes, features, and matrix.mtx.gz for GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1; create Seurat object with min.cells=3, min.features=200.

steps.title: Add QC metrics (nFeature_RNA, nCount_RNA, percent.mt)
steps.description: Calculate percent.mt using MT- gene prefix; inspect distributions to set filtering thresholds.

steps.title: Subset based on QC thresholds
steps.description: Filter cells on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt (typical: nFeature 500–5000, percent.mt < 10%).

steps.title: Normalize, find HVGs, scale, and run PCA
Find neighbors, cluster, and run UMAP
Find all markers and visualize
steps.description: FindAllMarkers with test.use='wilcox'; show UMAP colored by cluster and top 3 markers per cluster.

assumptions: Using mitochondrial gene prefix 'MT-' (standard for mammalian 10x).
Using Wilcoxon rank-sum test for marker detection.
